description: Close the edge-orientation ambiguity class (FGAP-113): make edge orientation explicit metadata on every orientation-bearing relation_type and consume it symmetrically so a wrong-orientation guess is never silent on either side. WRITE — a backwards orientation-bearing edge is REJECTED at append (never auto-oriented; the reverted 2026-06-06 always-swap inverted canonically-authored edges, a08e586). READ — a walk/query issued against the wrong end of an orientation-bearing relation ERRORS (or normalizes with an explicit signal) rather than returning silent-empty. Covers ordering relations (task_depends_on_task and its same-kind siblings), the membership relations (reconciling the contains/includes vs positioned_in container-orientation split), and the unconstrained wildcard item_derived_from_item; reconciles relation_type names/metadata with the deriver contract; migrates any inverted edges. FGAP-007 (task_depends_on_task) is the write-ordering instance; the task_positioned_in_phase silent-empty is the read instance.
acceptance_criteria: Every orientation-bearing relation_type carries explicit endpoint-role/orientation metadata (extending the existing source_kinds/target_kinds) naming which stored endpoint holds which semantic role: the ordering relations (task_depends_on_task, story_depends_on_story, feature_depends_on_item, feature_gated_by_item, story_gated_by_item, requirement_depends_on_requirement, milestone_precedes_milestone, decision_supersedes_decision, research_supersedes_research), the membership relations (feature_contains_story, story_includes_item, task_positioned_in_phase, phase_positioned_in_milestone), and the wildcard item_derived_from_item — each either carries the metadata or is demonstrably exempt because it has no consumed orientation semantics; none is left with a name-vs-deriver or name-vs-container inversion.

WRITE enforcement: append-relation/append-relations REJECT a backwards orientation-bearing edge at write time with a message naming the expected orientation — a direction-correct edge is accepted, a backwards one is rejected, never silently stored and never auto-oriented. The rejection fires only for relation_types carrying the orientation metadata; relations without it are unaffected (no new false-positive rejections). Provable per relation_type: a name-faithful-but-backwards append is rejected; the canonical-direction append succeeds.

WRITE enforcement covers the wildcard: item_derived_from_item (source/target [*], no kind pin) is oriented by its declared role metadata, not by the absent kind check — a backwards item_derived_from_item append is rejected.

READ signaling: a walk-ancestors/walk-descendants (and any walk porcelain) issued against the WRONG end of an orientation-bearing relation ERRORS or returns an explicit wrong-orientation signal — not a bare empty array indistinguishable from a genuine absence. Provable: querying task_positioned_in_phase from the member end vs the container end yields distinguishable results (one the membership, the other an explicit wrong-direction signal), never two silently-different empties.

Membership orientation is reconciled to internal consistency: the container-orientation split (contains/includes store container=parent while positioned_in stores container=child) is resolved or the metadata explicitly encodes each so no learned heuristic is silently wrong for a sibling; documented on the relation_type surface.

Every existing edge in relations.json remains valid after the fix and any migration: none orphaned, dropped, or left inverted; any edge whose stored direction is backwards under the corrected contract is migrated to correct orientation; already-correct edges are unchanged.
The deriver's blocked/ready and lineage output is unchanged where edges were already correct and corrected only where an edge was inverted: no correctly-derived outcome changes.

context-validate (and context-validate-relations) passes against the active substrate after the fix and after any edge migration, with no new integrity findings; optionally a validate-time check flags any orientation-bearing edge whose stored direction violates its declared roles.

Tests assert both sides: a backwards ordering edge rejected + forwards accepted; the wildcard item_derived_from_item orientation enforced; the metadata-absent gate holds (no enforcement when orientation metadata is absent); a wrong-end read errors/signals rather than returning silent-empty; the deriver continues to treat {parent=prerequisite, child=dependent} correctly.
Build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.
